decisions: Defect express lane: phases 2-3 skipped per manifest scope 'defect'; implemented directly from 01-business-analyst.json ACs.

Regression test written as a zero-dependency static source assertion (scripts/test-halt-message-paths.cjs) reading both source files, because the defect is message text; runner is node (Node project), not phpunit.
Revert-check protocol followed exactly: regression test staged via git add, both fixes left as unstaged working-tree changes so the engine could stash-revert them.
Fixes left uncommitted per phase instructions; commit is a later-phase/human decision.

notes: COVERAGE DECISION (sole failure reason): coverage unverifiable - no JS coverage driver installed (verified: no node_modules; jest declared in devDependencies but not installed; no c8/nyc; .cjs suite is not jest-based). Per handshake spec no-coverage-driver clause this is a tooling blocker, not a quality FAIL: install a driver (npm install + wire c8/nyc around the node test scripts) or a human may waive. Gate cannot waive on its own initiative; the 'intent satisfied by 2/2 changed lines asserted' argument was weighed and rejected as a self-initiated waiver, which the spec forbids. QA phase honesty confirmed: NO fabricated coverage number anywhere in 05-qa-engineer.json or docs/qa/KEEL-101-qa-report.md; '2/2 changed lines asserted' is a countable diff fact, transparently labeled analysis. ALL EXECUTABLE CLAIMS REPRODUCED BY GATE: node scripts/test-keel-state.cjs = 11/11 pass exit 0 (matches claim); node scripts/test-halt-message-paths.cjs = PASS exit 0 (matches claim). AC MAPPING VERIFIED IN TEST SOURCE: AC-1 asserted at test lines 18-25 against keel-watch.cjs 'HALTED' line (unique, line 96 = the changed line); AC-2 at test lines 27-34 against keel-state.cjs 'Keel pipeline HALTED' (unique, line 156 = the changed line); git diff -U0 confirms exactly 2 changed message-string lines, both containing the required resume path; both assertions bind to the exact changed lines. If a human waives coverage, re-gate PASS is warranted on the existing evidence with the waiver recorded verbatim.
coverage waived by human: 'waive coverage for KEEL-101 — 2-line message fix, revert-check proven'; all other claims verified by execution at attempt 1

decisions: Applied the scanner stack to the actual stack: composer audit and PHPStan recorded skipped (not applicable — Node project) instead of pretending PHP baselines ran.

Treated the initial npm audit ENOLOCK as fixable tooling rather than a FAILED gate: generated an ephemeral lockfile with --package-lock-only --ignore-scripts (no code installed, no scripts run), ran the audit for real (0 vulnerabilities), then deleted the lockfile to restore the tree — recorded the full method and its point-in-time caveat as a LOW finding instead of hiding it.

Rated the interpolated-state-values-in-instructional-messages pattern INFO not LOW/MEDIUM: it predates this diff, data is local-repo-controlled, and output is terminal/Slack text with no execution sink.
Did not block on the missing codegraph: blast-radius baseline is optional-when-present per spec; substituted a manual consumer check of the two changed message literals.
